heading: Grants to improve the commercial value of forest biomass for electric energy, useful heat, transportation fuels, and other commercial purposes
label: 15855 Grants to improve the commercial value of forest biomass for electric energy, useful heat, transportation fuels, and other commercial purposes
notes: Amendments2006—Subsec. (d). substituted “$50,000,000 for fiscal year 2006 and $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2016” for “$50,000,000 for each of the fiscal years 2006 through 2016”.Pub. L. 109–375

children.label: A

children.label: B

children.label: C

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 2 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304(e) of title 25

children.heading: Nonmerchantable
children.label: 3 Nonmerchantable
children.text: For purposes of subsection (b), the term “nonmerchantable” means that portion of the byproducts of preventive treatments that would not otherwise be used for higher value products.

children.heading: Secretary concerned
children.label: 6 Secretary concerned
children.text: The term “Secretary concerned” means the Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of the Interior.

children.heading: Biomass commercial use grant program
children.label: b Biomass commercial use grant program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary concerned may make grants to any person in a preferred community that owns or operates a facility that uses biomass as a raw material to produce electric energy, sensible heat, or transportation fuels to offset the costs incurred to purchase biomass for use by such facility.

children.heading: Grant amounts
children.label: 2 Grant amounts
children.text: A grant under this subsection may not exceed $20 per green ton of biomass delivered.

children.heading: Monitoring of grant recipient activities
children.label: 3 Monitoring of grant recipient activities
children.text: As a condition of a grant under this subsection, the grant recipient shall keep such records as the Secretary concerned may require to fully and correctly disclose the use of the grant funds and all transactions involved in the purchase of biomass. Upon notice by a representative of the Secretary concerned, the grant recipient shall afford the representative reasonable access to the facility that purchases or uses biomass and an opportunity to examine the inventory and records of the facility.

children.heading: Improved biomass use grant program
children.label: c Improved biomass use grant program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary concerned may make grants to persons to offset the cost of projects to develop or research opportunities to improve the use of, or add value to, biomass. In making such grants, the Secretary concerned shall give preference to persons in preferred communities.

children.label: A
children.text: the anticipated public benefits of the project, including the potential to develop thermal or electric energy resources or affordable energy;

children.label: B
children.text: opportunities for the creation or expansion of small businesses and micro-businesses;

children.label: C
children.text: the potential for new job creation;

children.label: D
children.text: the potential for the project to improve efficiency or develop cleaner technologies for biomass utilization; and

children.label: E
children.text: the potential for the project to reduce the hazardous fuels from the areas in greatest need of treatment.

children.heading: Grant amount
children.label: 3 Grant amount
children.text: A grant under this subsection may not exceed $500,000.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: d Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated $50,000,000 for fiscal year 2006 and $35,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2007 through 2016 to carry out this section.
An identification of the size, type, and use of biomass by persons that receive grants under this section.
The distance between the land from which the biomass was removed and the facility that used the biomass.
The economic impacts, particularly new job creation, resulting from the grants to and operation of the eligible operations.
